questions.question: An MIMD system has a processor that can execute 1000 instructions per second and another processor that can execute 1000 instructions per second. The two processors can run in parallel. According to Amdahl's law, what is the maximum theoretical speedup that can be achieved in this system? Assume that the serial component is 0.1 seconds. What would be the actual speedup if the serial component was 0.5 seconds?

A program running on a MIMD system has a parallel component that can execute in 10 seconds and a serial component that can execute in 2 seconds. According to Amdahl's law, what is the maximum theoretical speedup that can be achieved by this program?

A MIMD system consists of 4 processors, each with a clock speed of 2 GHz. The system is used to run a program that has a parallel component that can execute in 1 second and a serial component that can execute in 0.1 seconds. Taking timings into account, what is the actual speedup achieved by the program?
